description.en: A rustic wheat sourdough from the saloio farm belt ringing Lisbon, where smallholders once carted bread and produce into the capital daily. A long autolyse, three folds and a full day's cold retard build real sourdough depth under a deeply golden, crackling crust.
description.de: Ein rustikales Weizensauerteigbrot aus dem saloio-Bauerngürtel rund um Lissabon, aus dem Kleinbauern einst täglich Brot in die Hauptstadt karrten. Autolyse, drei Falten und ein ganzer Tag kalte Gare sorgen für echte Sauerteigtiefe unter goldbrauner, krachender Kruste.
description.es: Un pan de masa madre de trigo rústico de la franja agrícola saloia que rodea Lisboa, desde donde los pequeños agricultores llevaban antaño pan y productos a diario a la capital. Autolisis larga, tres pliegues y un día de frío dan una profundidad real bajo una corteza dorada.
description.fr: Un pain au levain de blé rustique de la ceinture agricole saloia qui entoure Lisbonne, d'où les petits paysans amenaient jadis pain et produits en ville chaque jour. Autolyse longue, trois rabats et une journée de froid donnent une vraie profondeur sous une croûte bien dorée.

origin.note: 'Saloio' names the smallholder farmers who historically supplied Lisbon with bread, produce and dairy from the surrounding countryside.

keeping.note: A long cold ferment gives real sourdough depth and decent keeping power for a few days.

method.title: Autolyse
method.summary: Mix the flour and water and rest 30 minutes before adding anything else.

method.title: Mix in the starter
method.summary: Combine with the sourdough starter and knead about 8 minutes, then work in the salt.

method.title: Fold to build strength
method.summary: Give the dough three sets of stretch-and-folds at 30-minute intervals, then rest 90 minutes.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Pre-shape into a rough round, rest 30 minutes, then shape tightly into a ball.

method.title: Cold retard
method.summary: Place seam-up in a floured banneton and refrigerate up to 24 hours for flavor and an easy schedule.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 250°C with convection for about 50 minutes until deeply golden.

source.adaptation: Starter assumed roughly 100% hydration to split into total-flour percentages.
